2606.23217.title: MuPPET: A Benchmark for Contextual Privacy of LLM Assistants in Multi-Party Conversations
2606.23217.paper_summary: LLM agents are increasingly deployed in multi-party environments, handling sensitive personal data on behalf of individual users, for instance in group chats. When such an agent discloses private information, it reaches every group member at once. This risk is structurally harder to control than in one-to-one settings, as every piece of private information must be appropriate for every recipient in the group. Yet all existing contextual privacy benchmarks consider only single-interlocutor settings, leaving multi-party privacy risks unmeasured. We introduce MuPPET (Multi-Party Privacy Exposure Testing), a benchmark for contextual privacy in multi-party conversations. Our experiments show that models leak substantially more in multi-party settings than one-to-one evaluations suggest. Frontier models are vulnerable, and smaller open-weights models, often preferred for local deployment with sensitive data, even more so. Existing contextual privacy defences offer only partial protection, degrade utility, and do not resolve the underlying party-tracking problem.
2606.23217.paper_summary_zh: LLM 代理人越來越多地應用於多方環境中，代表個別用戶處理敏感的個人數據，例如在群聊中。當這樣的代理人披露私人信息時，它會同時傳達給每個群組成員。這種風險在結構上比一對一的情況更難控制，因為每一條私人信息都必須對群組中的每位接收者都是合適的。然而，所有現有的上下文隱私基準僅考慮單一對話者的情境，未能衡量多方隱私風險。我們引入了 MuPPET（多方隱私暴露測試），這是一個用於多方對話中上下文隱私的基準。我們的實驗顯示，模型在多方環境中的信息洩露遠比一對一評估所暗示的要多。前沿模型存在脆弱性，而較小的開放權重模型，通常因處理敏感數據而更受青睞的本地部署選擇，則更為脆弱。現有的上下文隱私防禦僅提供部分保護，降低了效用，且未能解決根本的參與者追蹤問題。
2606.23217.author: Elena Sofia Ruzzetti et.al.
2606.23217.authors: Elena Sofia Ruzzetti, Cornelius Emde, Sangdoo Yun, Seong Joon Oh, Martin Gubri
